acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All 12 IPC protocol types defined in beads-surface
- [ ] No `impl Into<...>` on cross-crate boundaries
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-surface` passes
- [ ] Types use beads_core:: and beads_api:: imports
title: Phase 3B: Move IPC protocol types to beads-surface

description:
**Problem**
Phase 2 tests need canonical CBOR fixtures and apply test harness.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §18: canonical hashing stability, apply idempotence
- Blocks Phase 2 testing bead

**Design**
Create test infrastructure:
- Golden CBOR fixtures: known EventBody → known sha256 (cross-platform stable)
- EventBody test factories with deterministic stamps/IDs
- Apply test harness: CanonicalState setup + apply + verify helpers
- Fingerprint comparison utilities

**Acceptance**
- [ ] tests/fixtures/cbor.rs with golden CBOR bytes + expected hashes
- [ ] tests/fixtures/event_body.rs with EventBody builders
- [ ] tests/fixtures/apply_harness.rs with apply + verify helpers
- [ ] Golden fixtures verified manually on multiple platforms (macOS, Linux)

**Files:** tests/fixtures/cbor.rs (new), tests/fixtures/event_body.rs (new), tests/fixtures/apply_harness.rs (new)
title: Phase 2 test infrastructure: CBOR fixtures + apply harness

closed_reason: Root-cause identified as benchmark startup contamination in log-derived summaries; harness now captures measured-phase log boundary after warm-up so ready outlier no longer appears in steady-state store identity summaries (artifact: tmp/perf/hotpaths-20260212-161756).
description:
Problem: ipc_request_duration histogram for ready shows rare outlier (~480ms max) despite p95 around 1ms after cache fix.

Design:
- Capture precise cause path (read-gate wait, scheduler tick, or sync/checkpoint interaction).
- Add targeted instrumentation around ready request lifecycle when latency > threshold.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Repro scenario documented
- [ ] Root-cause identified with logs/metrics
- [ ] Mitigation landed or follow-up implementation bead filed
title: Investigate rare >400ms ready outlier under hotpath benchmark

closed_reason: Fixed PR #88 follow-up regressions: bootstrap repo discovery now preserves GIT_DIR/GIT_WORK_TREE semantics, daemon config reload uses full-file precedence semantics again, and config test env isolation is centralized through beads_bootstrap::config::CONFIG_ENV_KEYS. Verified with cargo fmt --all --check, cargo test -p beads-bootstrap, cargo test -p beads-daemon --test config_precedence -- --nocapture, cargo test -p beads-daemon --doc, and cargo check -p beads-rs --all-features.
title: Fix post-review regressions in repo discovery and daemon config semantics on PR #88
